headline: GOLD HITS ONE-MONTH LOW AT $4,554 AS FED MEETS, IRAN TALKS COLLAPSE
snippet: Gold fell to its weakest level since early April as stalled US-Iran peace negotiations prolonged the Strait of Hormuz closure, stoking inflation fears and eroding rate-cut expectations on the day of the Federal Reserve's policy decision.

headline: SILVER DROPS TO $73, TESTS KEY $72 SUPPORT AMID SIXTH STRAIGHT SUPPLY DEFICIT
snippet: Silver broke below a key ascending channel to test $72 support, pressured by rising inflation expectations and Fed hold bets — even as structural supply fundamentals confirm the market is heading into its sixth consecutive annual deficit in 2026.

headline: MINING M&A HITS $21.6B IN Q1 — STRONGEST START TO A YEAR SINCE 2023
snippet: Global mining deal value surged 34% year-on-year to $21.6 billion across 121 transactions in Q1 2026, as critical-mineral demand and record gold prices sustained the consolidation wave that pushed full-year 2025 M&A to a 13-year high of $93.7 billion.

headline: LUCA MINING DRILLS 118M AT 2.53 G/T GOLD AND 78 G/T SILVER AT CAMPO MORADO VMS MINE
snippet: Luca Mining (TSXV: LUCA) reported 118.2m of 2.53 g/t Au, 78 g/t Ag, 0.8% Cu and 2.0% Zn in the previously unmined Largo Norte zone at its producing Campo Morado polymetallic mine in Mexico, materially expanding the VMS footprint beyond the current resource.

headline: ARGENTA SILVER HITS 1,385 G/T AG OVER 4M, DISCOVERS NEW HIGH-GRADE ZONE AT EL QUEVAR
snippet: Argenta Silver (TSXV: AGAG) intersected 1,385 g/t Ag over 4.0m at Mani-Copan and discovered a new mineralized zone at the Argentina target (725 g/t Ag over 3.0m) — both located 800–1,500m outside the current 45.3 Moz Ag resource at El Quevar, Argentina.

headline: NEVADA KING DOUBLES DRILL PROGRAM TO 40,000M AFTER 20.8 G/T AU SURFACE HIT, BLM APPROVES 33 NEW SITES
snippet: Nevada King Gold doubled its Phase 4 RC drill program at the Atlanta Mine in Nevada to 40,000m after the BLM approved 33 new drill sites in an area where a 20.8 g/t Au rock chip — the project's highest-ever surface sample — was recently collected.

headline: KODIAK COPPER LAUNCHES 6,500M PORPHYRY DRILL CAMPAIGN AT BC'S MPD PROJECT
snippet: Kodiak Copper (TSXV: KDK) kicked off a fully-funded 6,500m core drill program at the MPD copper-gold porphyry in BC's Quesnel Terrane, targeting the untested Ketchan deposit alongside IP and AMT geophysics across the seven-deposit project.

headline: CHILE'S COPPER OUTPUT IN JEOPARDY AS CHINA'S SULPHURIC ACID EXPORT BAN STARTS MAY 1
snippet: China's ban on sulphuric acid exports takes effect May 1, threatening Chile's 1.1 million tonnes of annual leached copper output — roughly 20% of global supply — with Morgan Stanley warning that no alternative supplier can fill the gap at scale and Goldman Sachs flagging Chile and the DRC as most exposed.

headline: USA RARE EARTH'S $2.8B BRAZIL BUYOUT CREATES FIRST SCALED RARE EARTH PRODUCER OUTSIDE ASIA
snippet: USA Rare Earth's $2.8B acquisition of Serra Verde's Pela Ema mine in Brazil delivers all four critical magnet rare earths (Nd, Pr, Tb, Dy) with a government-backed offtake and management projecting $300–650M EBITDA by end-2027 — giving the U.S. its first credible non-China rare earth supply chain.

headline: RARE EARTH STOCKS GAIN AS CHINA PRICES SURGE 45% IN A SINGLE QUARTER, SUPPLY CHAIN RACE ACCELERATES
snippet: Chinese rare earth concentrate prices jumped 45% quarter-on-quarter to 38,804 yuan/tonne in Q2 2026, driving broad gains in Western producers including MP Materials and Aclara Resources as investors bet on accelerating supply chain diversification away from China.

headline: GOLD MINERS TRADING CHEAP VS. THE METAL IN 'STRUCTURAL' BULL MARKET — EQUINOX GOLD TOP PICK: HAYWOOD
snippet: Haywood Securities argues senior gold producers remain cheap at 9.6x forward cash flow — barely above the five-year average — despite record margins and expanding free cash flow, calling pullbacks attractive entry points and flagging Equinox Gold as the top pick for Canadian production growth.

headline: FED RATE CUT NOW SEEN IN LATE 2026 AS WAR-DRIVEN INFLATION FORCES ECONOMISTS TO RETHINK: REUTERS POLL
snippet: A Reuters survey of economists finds rate cut expectations have been pushed firmly to late 2026 or 2027 as the Strait of Hormuz closure sustains elevated oil and energy prices that complicate the Fed's inflation mandate — a persistent headwind for gold's near-term recovery despite record-high prices.
